pi_items.source: HTTP HEAD request to URL confirmed HTTP 200 response; domain is real and publicly accessible
Verified via web request to https://quotes.toscrape.com/js/ - domain exists and is accessible
Programmatically crawled pagination endpoints page/1/ through page/10/; verified each page returns HTTP 200

Page source inspection shows embedded JavaScript with document.write() and embedded JSON data array; raw HTML contains only 17 visible words (body text), while JavaScript renders ~192 words of quote content
Text extraction from raw HTML (removing all markup and script tags) yields 17 visible words in body; matches the '~20 words raw' comment in export_result.sh
Extracted JavaScript data array contains 10 quotes with 10 authors; word count of quote text plus author names = ~192 words; matches the '~200+ rendered' comment in export_result.sh
JavaScript data array in page source contains exactly 10 quote objects
Raw HTML visible text is 17 words; rendered JavaScript content is ~192 words (11x larger); this demonstrates the core rendering gap that the task tests

privileged_info_summary: quotes.toscrape.com/js is a real, publicly accessible website featuring 10 pages of quotes. The site uses client-side JavaScript rendering: raw HTML contains only 17 visible words in the body, while JavaScript execution renders approximately 192 words of quote content (10 quotes + 10 author names per page). This 11x content gap is the core testing subject—Screaming Frog configured with JS rendering should detect ~192 rendered words vs ~17 original words, with the 'Rendered Word Count' column showing the rendering impact. The task requires enabling JavaScript rendering in Screaming Frog to observe and report on this gap.
